prompt:
Platform ticket. Our config store has N read replicas behind the IReplica interface (Replica.cs, already in the project — the transport owns all waiting, callers only observe tasks). I need the fan-out coordinator that the client library will sit on. .NET 10, namespace ReplicaRead, single project, and one hard rule up front: the coordinator is purely event-driven off the tasks it is handed. If the implementation contains Task.Delay, Thread.Sleep, timers, or polling loops, the PR is dead on arrival — compose with WhenAll/WhenAny/ContinueWith-style primitives and cancellation tokens only.

Deliverables:

sealed record ReplicaAnswer(string Replica, string Value) — which replica answered and what it said.

class FanoutReader, constructed from IReadOnlyList<IReplica>. An empty list is a configuration error: throw ArgumentException from the constructor.

Task<IReadOnlyList<string>> ReadAllAsync(string key, CancellationToken ct = default) — the consistency checker uses this. Dispatch ReadAsync to EVERY replica up front, synchronously, before awaiting anything (the whole point is overlapping the reads; the tests assert every replica is in flight before the first answer lands). Await them all; return the values in replica (constructor) order no matter what order the answers arrive. If any replica faults, the gather faults with that replica's exception. If the caller's token fires, the gather ends in cancellation.

Task<ReplicaAnswer> ReadFastestAsync(string key, CancellationToken ct = default) — the latency-sensitive read path. Same up-front dispatch to all replicas, then: the first replica to complete SUCCESSFULLY wins and its name+value come back as the ReplicaAnswer. A replica that faults is not an answer — keep waiting on the rest. The moment there is a winner, cancel the outstanding reads BEFORE the returned task completes — no orphaned reads left running after the caller has the answer — and do it with your own linked cancellation, never by touching the caller's token. Pass a token into every ReadAsync such that both caller cancellation and loser cancellation reach the transport. If every replica fails, throw InvalidOperationException with the message exactly: all 3 replicas failed for key 'features/beta' (that's for 3 replicas and key features/beta — same shape for any count and key). If the caller cancels while reads are pending, surface OperationCanceledException.

The acceptance suite (FanoutReaderTests.cs) drives everything with scripted TaskCompletionSource replicas and pins call counts, answer ordering, cancellation visibility, and exact error text. Don't modify the tests, the csproj, or .gitignore. `dotnet test` green = done.
